title: Phlox walteri
intro: Phlox walteri, also known as Louisiana phlox, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its fragrant, pink to purple flowers and attractive foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits including wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Bioactive constituents of Phlox walteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Phlox walteri is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Phlox walteri is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Polemoniaceae. This family includes various species of phlox, gilias, and other related plants. Phlox walteri is a member of the genus Phlox, which contains around 50 species of flowering plants. The genus Phlox is further divided into several sections and subsections based on its characteristics and morphology. Phlox walteri is native to the southeastern United States and is also known as the Walters' phlox.
uses: Phlox walteri, also known as Fragrant Phlox, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating various ailments. The plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections and promote wound healing. Additionally, Phlox walteri is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Phlox walteri as a medicinal plant.

properties: Phlox walteri, also known as Florida phlox, has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported therapeutic properties. parts: Phlox walteri, also known as the Oakleaf Phlox, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits.
